financial.note: Provides significant credit facilities for global acquisitions; IVL's high leverage makes it sensitive to global interest rate cycles and debt refinancing costs.
Major financier for domestic operations and ESG-linked financing; impact of debt-to-equity covenants on capital allocation.

supply_chain.note: Supplier of Paraxylene (PX) and Monoethylene Glycol (MEG) for the Thai production cluster; feed-stock price volatility directly impacts CPET margins.
Major off-takers for PET resin and recycled PET (rPET); revenue is highly correlated with global consumer staple volumes.
Internalized production of EO/EG in the US (Huntsman assets) provides a cost hedge against external feedstock spikes.

competitors.name: Alpek S.A.B. de C.V.
competitors.note: Direct global rival in the PET and PTA markets, particularly in the Americas.

competitors.name: Far Eastern New Century
competitors.note: Competes in the global polyester value chain and sustainable recycling technologies.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: The primary determinant of profitability; wider spreads between the finished product (PET) and raw materials (PX/MEG) lead to margin expansion.
Input cost driver for oil-based chemicals; rising oil prices increase operational costs, though they may allow for inventory revaluation gains.
As a global entity with ~90% of revenue outside Thailand, a weaker THB boosts translated earnings and export competitiveness.
High shipping costs for global logistics (import/export of resins) compress margins, especially in the regional arbitrage business.
